top_signals.title: Google Loses EU Antitrust Fight; Softbank Moves on Computing Power | Stock Movers
top_signals.summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers:

- SoftBank (SFTBY) shares are responding to news that it is starting a new U.S. venture to rent out the computing power needed to build and run artificial-intelligence models, aiming to capitalize on strong American demand for AI computing resources.
- Shares of Alphabet/Google (GOOG) are responding to the company losing its long-running fight against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) European Union antitrust fine after the bloc’s top judges said regulators were right to punish the US giant for abusing Android’s market power. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: AI Debt Deluge Makes Credit Market Look Safer While Masking Risk
top_signals.summary: On paper, the US corporate bond market is getting safer as more high-rated tech companies sell debt. Many investors fear that safety will prove illusory.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: SAS CEO Emerges as Possible Air Canada Boss After French Debacle
top_signals.summary: SAS AB Chief Executive Officer Anko van der Werff is emerging as a frontrunner to lead Air Canada, according to people familiar with the matter, succeeding Michael Rousseau, who said in March he’d leave after a public outcry over the his omission to speak French in a corporate video.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: National Payments Vision
top_signals.summary: The government has published the National Payments Vision, setting out its ambitions for the UK’s payments sector to deliver world-leading payments and support the growth mission.
